suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: For corridor/revocation flows (password change, password reset, etc.), workers should be aware that signOut() in AuthContext handles already-revoked sessions gracefully via try/catch with finally block for storage clearing. This pattern ensures clean state even when backend has already revoked the session.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: auth-recovery-corridor-reauth handoff noted: 'The skill procedure could mention that for corridor/revocation flows, it's important to verify the signOut behavior in AuthContext handles already-revoked sessions gracefully (it does via try/catch with finally block for storage clearing).'

rejectedObservations.observation: qrcode.react library added as a new dependency for QR code rendering
rejectedObservations.reason: Informational only - dependency additions are captured in package.json and don't need library documentation

rejectedObservations.observation: Discriminated union error types (lockout, runtime_denial) in error-resolver.ts
rejectedObservations.reason: Internal implementation detail - the pattern is self-documenting in code and not a cross-cutting convention needing library documentation

rejectedObservations.observation: Error discrimination pattern in ResetPasswordPage (checking for 'password'/'policy' in message)
rejectedObservations.reason: Page-specific implementation detail - not a reusable pattern that needs to be propagated to library

reviewDetails.summary: MFA verification correctly re-submits POST /auth/login with original credentials, login denial states are properly differentiated (credential failures, lockout, runtime denials), MFA setup/disable flows complete with QR code, recovery codes, and both TOTP and recovery code paths. 77 targeted tests, all passing.

issues.description: Minor eslint-disable comment for react-hooks/exhaustive-deps on auto-submit effect
MFA disable dialog uses inline styles for animation rather than Tailwind

reviewDetails.summary: Forgot-password uses only canonical path with proper persistence failure detection, reset-password distinguishes token errors from password-policy errors, must-change-password corridor forces fresh login after password change. 11 new tests covering VAL-AUTH-011 through VAL-AUTH-014 and VAL-CROSS-003.
